title: Repay complexity budget and decompose installer boundaries
rawUserRequest: 继续实施 2026-07-22 Conditional GO Review Remediation 全部计划，严格按顺序完成当前工单、PR、合并、ai-close-work-item 和后续工单，最终发布新版本。当前只进入 WI10，不跳过任何计划工单。

declaredIntent.summary: 先交付可量化的复杂度偿还，再将 install_ai_cockpit.py 的职责拆到 repository、detection、transaction、ownership、bootstrap、upgrade、managed_regions、evidence 模块，并保留薄入口。

outOfScope: Skipping any earlier or later Work Item.
Raising complexity limits without an actual repayment record.
Changing adopter runtime behavior outside installer boundaries and their regression tests.

sources.reason: Approved plan Work Item 10 objective and acceptance.
Repository Work Item lifecycle, scope, and complexity governance rules.
Canonical governance and Work Item terminology.
Current installer monolith and public entry point to decompose.
Measured complexity report and repayment metrics.
Blocking complexity budget and repayment policy.

riskAssessment.reason: The change moves installer responsibilities across modules and modifies complexity evidence; compatibility and budget accounting must be verified.

executionDecision.reason: The predecessor Work Item is closed, the default branch is synchronized, and the user authorized serial implementation of the complete plan.

preReviewWarnings: Review must confirm the installer entry point remains behavior-compatible, each new module has a single verifiable boundary, the repayment is measurable, and no complexity budget is increased without repayment evidence.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: A repayment record demonstrates a measured reduction or consolidation before any net complexity increase is accepted.

install_ai_cockpit.py remains a thin compatible entry point while installer responsibilities are separated into tested repository, detection, transaction, ownership, bootstrap, upgrade, managed-regions, and evidence boundaries.
Installer and module regression tests pass, including installed runtime parity and failure/rollback behavior.
Complexity report, policy/repayment evidence, architecture documentation, and Work Item Summary agree.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.
Keep the installer entry point compatible and fail closed on transaction or ownership errors.
Do not increase a blocking complexity limit without a concrete repayment record.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Existing installer CLI and public Installer behavior remain compatible through the thin entry point.
Repository detection, transaction, ownership, and evidence boundaries reject unsafe or partial installation states.
Measured complexity repayment is recorded and the complexity checker fails closed on unapproved budget growth.

intent.problem: The installer is a 1,800-line monolith and the repository has approached its Python complexity budget. Continuing to add lifecycle behavior without repayment makes the governance budget meaningless and weakens independent verification of installer boundaries.
constraints: Complete WI10 only after WI9 closure and from the synchronized default branch.
Preserve existing installer CLI and behavior unless a regression test documents an intentional boundary change.
Do not implement WI11 or publish the final release in this Work Item.
intent.rationale: A measured repayment paired with thin, tested module boundaries makes future installer changes reviewable without hiding complexity growth behind a higher limit.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the complete Conditional GO remediation plan, including governance policy repayment records and the full WI10 PR lifecycle.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
